task: I’m planning a quick beach walk this weekend and want to make sure I’m not missing anything important. Can you help me make a simple go/no-go checklist for what to look at before I leave and when I get there?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a simple go/no-go checklist for a beach walk:

## Before you leave
**Go if:**
- Tide is low or safely manageable for the area you’re going
- Waves look calm on the forecast
- Wind isn’t too strong
- Weather is clear enough for a short walk
- You have shoes with decent grip
- You know where you’re parking and roughly where you’ll walk

**No-go or rethink if:**
- Tide is high or rising fast
- Waves are breaking hard on rocks or the shore
- There’s a big swell, storm, or strong wind
- The path/shoreline you want to use is known to get cut off
- You’re not sure about the area and it looks exposed

## When you get there
**Go if:**
- You check the tide chart and it still looks okay
- You look at the water first and it seems calm enough
- Rocks, wet sand, and waves look stable and not sneaky
- You can clearly see your exit route back

**No-go or back up if:**
- Waves are reaching farther up than expected
- Rocks are slippery or getting splashed
- The shoreline looks narrower than you thought
- You see people turning back for safety
- Anything feels off and you’d have to guess your way through it

## Quick rule
If **tide + waves + rocks** all look good, go.  
If **any one of those looks sketchy**, skip the risky part or choose a simpler stretch.

If you want, I can turn this into a tiny one-line checklist you can save on your phone.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, this works. It’s simple and not too much. I’d probably save the quick rule on my phone. Also, the part about checking the exit route is good — I almost forgot that from the time I slipped near the tide pools. So, yeah, this feels useful.
